turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some pretty bad abdominal pain and bloody stools for the past three weeks, and it's really starting to affect my work and daily life. I'm getting worried that something more serious is going on.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain - it's mostly on my left side, but sometimes it feels like it's all over. It's been constant for three weeks now, and it gets worse when I eat or even just move around.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 3 weeks ago, I'd say. At first, it was just a dull ache on my left side after eating, but over time it's gotten more intense and widespread. It feels like my whole lower abdomen is cramped up all the time now.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens pretty much every day, sometimes it's just a dull ache, other times it's a sharp pain that makes me double over. I'd say it wakes me up at least 5-6 times a night, but when I'm awake during the day, it's constant - there's always some level of discomfort or pain.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 out of 10 most days. There are times when it's more like a 6 or 7, but those are rare - usually it's just this steady, gnawing ache that I can't shake.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've also been having bloody stools. It started a few days after the abdominal pain began, and at first it was just occasional, but now it's pretty much every time I have a bowel movement. The blood is bright red and mixed with stool, which really freaks me out.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: The bloody stools are definitely something that's been worrying me - it's not just the pain anymore, but also this fear of what might be causing it. And yeah, the pain does seem to get worse when I eat or drink anything, even water, and sometimes it gets triggered by stress too - like if I'm in a meeting at work and someone asks me a tough question, my gut will start cramping up right away.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: No, I don't really experience any of those symptoms. The pain is just kind of... steady, and the bloody stools are what's been keeping me up at night. That's what's scaring me the most - I've never had anything like this before, and I'm worried it might be something serious.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, just my abdomen. Sometimes the pain radiates up into my back a bit, but that's about it. Like I said, the joint pain in my knees and ankles has been bothering me too, but I don't know if that's related to this or not. It feels like achy arthritis, but I've never really had any issues with my joints before.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, there's no swelling at all - it just feels like a dull ache in my knees and ankles. But to be honest, that's been going on for a while now, even before the abdominal pain started, so I'm not sure if it's related or not.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've actually been trying to manage my symptoms with some over-the-counter meds. I've been taking loperamide for the diarrhea, and sometimes ibuprofen for the joint pain in my knees and ankles. But like I said, it doesn't really seem to be helping much - if anything, the ibuprofen seems to make the abdominal pain worse.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any known medical conditions. My dad has type 2 diabetes, but my mom is healthy and I'm pretty sure that's not related to what I'm experiencing right now. I'm also a non-smoker and I've never used recreational drugs, so I'm trying not to worry about anything too serious.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I don't think so, no. To be honest, I haven't been to the doctor in over two years, and I've never really thought about getting my cholesterol checked before. Like I said, I'm pretty healthy otherwise, but this abdominal pain and bloody stools are really freaking me out, and I just want to get to the bottom of it.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I don't smoke at all, I've never even considered it. As for drinking, I do have a few beers most weeknights, usually 2-3, but over the past month or so, I've actually been drinking more because of stress from work and these symptoms. It's gotten up to 4-5 beers some nights, which is definitely more than usual for me.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet has been pretty irregular lately due to long work hours and stress. I've just been grabbing takeout or eating on the go most of the time, which isn't great for my gut, I'm sure. As for exercise, I try to stay active by going for walks during my lunch break, but it's not like I have a regular workout routine or anything.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, my sister has Crohn's disease, which is an inflammatory bowel condition, but that's about it. She was diagnosed at age 30, and I'm worried that maybe this could be something similar for me.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: No, aside from my sister's Crohn's disease, no one else in my family has had any major health issues that I know of. My mom is healthy and active, and my dad just has type 2 diabetes like I mentioned earlier.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I haven't seen a doctor in over two years, to be honest. Like I said, I've been trying to manage my symptoms on my own with some OTC meds, but it's getting to the point where I really need someone to take a closer look at what's going on.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. I've never had any allergic reactions or issues with medications that I'm aware of. But like I said, I have been self-medicating with loperamide and ibuprofen for the past few weeks, and while it hasn't seemed to help much, I haven't noticed any adverse effects either.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah. I did travel to Mexico a few months ago for a work conference, but I didn't have any GI issues during or after the trip. And I've been trying to remember if I'd eaten anything out of the ordinary before this all started, but nothing comes to mind. Just regular takeout and restaurant food, like usual.
